feed.title: Google Cloud: Firestore with MongoDB compatibility
feed.subtitle: Changelog for Google Cloud product: Firestore with MongoDB compatibility

entry.content: The maximum document size has been increased to 16 MiB. To learn more, see Behavior differences.
entry.summary: The maximum document size limit has been increased to 16 MiB. This change allows for larger data storage within documents. For more details on how this impacts behavior, please refer to the documentation on behavior differences.
entry.title: Feature: Document size limit increased

entry.content:
Support for text and geospatial search. You can create text indexes, perform text and geospatial search queries using the $text and $near operators, handle language settings, and calculate relevance scores. To learn more, see Text search and Geospatial search.

These features are available in Preview.
entry.summary: Enhance your applications with powerful text and geospatial search features. You can now create text indexes, perform text and geospatial queries, manage language settings, and calculate relevance scores. These capabilities are available in preview.
entry.title: Feature: New Text and Geospatial Search Capabilities

entry.summary: The $lookup aggregation stage now includes support for the 'let' and 'pipeline' options. This enhancement provides more flexibility and power when performing correlated subqueries within your MongoDB aggregations.
entry.title: Feature: $lookup now supports let and pipeline

entry.content:
Support for Change Streams. Change streams let applications access real-time changes (inserts, updates, and deletes) made to a collection or to an entire database.

This feature is available in Preview.
entry.summary: Applications can now access real-time changes like inserts, updates, and deletes to collections or entire databases using Change Streams. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Real-time Data Change Streams Available

entry.content:
Support for the drop() command to delete entire collections.

This feature is available in Preview.
entry.summary: You can now delete entire collections using the `drop()` command. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Collection deletion support

entry.content: You can now use Gemini Code Assist to get AI-powered assistance in Firestore to generate MQL queries using natural language prompts. This feature is available in Preview.
entry.summary: Gemini Code Assist now offers AI-powered assistance for Firestore, allowing you to generate MQL queries using natural language prompts. This new feature, available in Preview, simplifies data querying in Firestore.
entry.title: Feature: AI-powered assistance for Firestore queries

entry.content: Support for managing TTL indexes in the MongoDB API.
entry.summary: You can now manage TTL indexes in the MongoDB API. This feature allows you to automatically remove documents from collections after a specified time. Learn more about how to create TTL indexes.
entry.title: Feature: TTL indexes support

entry.content:
The Firestore databases page in the Google Cloud console now includes a status column. Possible statuses include:

  • Ready
  • Cloning is in progress
  • Restoring from backup is in progress
  • Deleted
  • Failed

For the cloning and restore statuses, the status column updates upon completion.
entry.summary: The Firestore databases page now displays a status column, showing states like Ready, Cloning in progress, Restoring from backup, Deleted, or Failed. This column updates in real-time for cloning and restore operations.
entry.title: Feature: New status column in Firestore

entry.summary: You can now use objects as document identifiers. This feature enhances flexibility for unique document identification.
entry.title: Feature: Object ID Support
